title: Erigeron oharae
taxonomy: Erigeron oharae is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to Japan and is known to grow in rocky and mountainous areas. The plant is classified as a perennial, with small white or pink flowers that bloom in the summer months. Erigeron oharae is also known as Ohara's fleabane, and is a member of the Erigeron genus. The exact classification of the plant at the species level within the genus Erigeron is still being researched.

intro: Erigeron glabellus, also known as Ohara's fleabane or Erigeron oharae, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, with its benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron glabellus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Erigeron glabellus is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical applications.
